# WorldSpec — full single-file language reference (v0.1)

This one file contains everything an LLM needs to read or write conforming
WorldSpec: what it is, the complete language rules, the grammars, the canonical
IR, the diagnostics, and a complete worked example. Canonical spec:
https://w3id.org/worldspec/spec/0.1/

================================================================================
1. WHAT IT IS
================================================================================
WorldSpec is a declarative language for describing a software system (in any
language or product) as facts that can be validated and reasoned over before the
system is changed. A *model* describes a system with six constructs and compiles
to a neutral canonical IR. The language is technology-agnostic and imposes no
domain vocabulary; the modeler defines their own entities, rules, and actions.

A model is a directory of one or more .yaml/.yml documents, merged into one
namespace before validation. Construct names MUST be unique across the model.
The compiler never uses `eval`; expressions are structured ASTs.

================================================================================
2. THE SIX CONSTRUCTS
================================================================================
- entity        A thing that exists, with stable identity and properties.
- relationship  A typed, directed association between two entities.
- state         The mutable dimensions of an entity that change over time.
- invariant     A boolean rule over an entity that MUST always hold.
- action        A permitted change, with preconditions, effects, and rollback.
- transition    A larger move that applies an action and preserves invariants.

Key idea: `entity` properties are static; `state` dimensions are what changes.
Invariants and actions reason over state, so the things that can break are
exactly the things you declared as mutable.

================================================================================
3. DOCUMENT AND NAMING RULES
================================================================================
Each top-level mapping key has the form "<construct> <Name>".
- <construct> is one of the six lower-case keywords above.
- <Name> is UpperCamelCase (^[A-Z][A-Za-z0-9_]*$) for every construct EXCEPT
  relationship, whose names are lowerCamelCase (^[a-z][A-Za-z0-9_]*$).
- Property and state-dimension names are lowerCamelCase.
Malformed keys, unknown construct keywords, or bad name case -> WS-SYN-* errors.

================================================================================
4. TYPE SYSTEM
================================================================================
Closed type vocabulary for properties, inputs, and state dimensions:
  string    UTF-8 text
  int       integer
  float     floating point
  bool      boolean
  datetime  ISO-8601 timestamp
  enum      one value from a fixed list; REQUIRES  values: [ ... ]  (non-empty, unique)
  ref       reference to an entity instance; REQUIRES  target: <EntityName>
A typeDecl is a mapping, e.g.  { type: string, required: true }  or
{ type: enum, values: [low, high] }  or  { type: ref, target: Account }.
(`required` applies to properties/inputs; state dimensions are never required.)

================================================================================
5. CONSTRUCT SHAPES
================================================================================
entity <Name>:
  description: <text>            # optional
  identity: [ <propName>, ... ]  # required, non-empty; each MUST be a declared property
  properties:                    # required
    <propName>: <typeDecl>

relationship <name>:             # lowerCamelCase
  from: <Entity>                 # required, declared entity
  to: <Entity>                   # required, declared entity
  cardinality: one | many        # required
  temporal: <bool>               # optional, default false

state <Name>:
  entity: <Entity>               # required, declared entity
  dimensions:                    # required
    <dimName>: <typeDecl>

invariant <Name>:
  appliesTo: <Entity>            # required, declared entity
  expression: <expr>             # required (see section 6)
  severity: info | warning | high | critical   # required
  onViolation:                   # optional
    action: block_transition | warn | record   # default warn

action <Name>:
  inputs:                        # required
    <inputName>: { type: ref, target: <Entity> }   # typically refs
  preconditions:                 # optional, list of predicate strings (section 7)
    - "<predicate>"
  effects:                       # required, non-empty, list of assignment strings (section 7)
    - "<assignment>"
  rollback:                      # optional; if absent the action is irreversible (warning)
    - "<assignment>"

transition <Name>:
  action: <Action>               # required, declared action
  from: { ... }                  # optional, free-form context map
  to:   { ... }                  # optional, free-form context map
  preserves: [ <Invariant>, ... ]# optional, declared invariant names

================================================================================
6. INVARIANT EXPRESSION GRAMMAR (structured AST, never free text, never eval)
================================================================================
expression  = comparison | logical
comparison  = { operator: <cmp>, left: <operand>, right: <operand> }
logical     = { operator: <bool>, operands: [ <expression>, ... ] }   # `not` takes exactly one
operand     = <literal scalar> | { path: "<dotted.path>" } | { function: <fn>, path: "<dotted.path>" }
<cmp>       = "==" | "!=" | "<" | "<=" | ">" | ">="
<bool>      = and | or | not
<fn>        = count | sum | min | max | exists
Every path MUST resolve to a declared property or state dimension of the
invariant's appliesTo entity (else WS-SEM-0050).

================================================================================
7. ACTION PREDICATE / ASSIGNMENT MINI-LANGUAGE (single-line, safe, never eval)
================================================================================
predicate   = path <cmp> operand     e.g.  dataset.lockMode == "none"
assignment  = path "=" operand        e.g.  dataset.writeAuthority = target
operand     = path | string | number | bool
path        = ident ("." ident)*
Strings may be single- or double-quoted; true/false are booleans; numbers are
int/float. The leading ident of every path MUST be a declared input name of the
action (else WS-SEM-0031).

================================================================================
8. CANONICAL IR (the stable downstream contract)
================================================================================
Every construct compiles to a neutral JSON object with a `kind` discriminator.
The whole model compiles to:
  { "irVersion": "0.1.0", "model": "<name>",
    "constructs": [ { "kind": "...", "name": "...", ... }, ... ] }
Consumers depend on the IR, never on the raw YAML.

================================================================================
9. DIAGNOSTICS
================================================================================
WS-SYN-#### : syntax / structural errors (bad key, missing field, bad type,
              malformed expression, bad name case).
WS-SEM-#### : semantic errors (unknown reference, duplicate name, dangling
              invariant in a transition, identity property not declared, path
              that does not resolve, ...).
Any error-severity diagnostic fails validation with a non-zero exit code.

================================================================================
10. CONFORMANCE (summary)
================================================================================
A conforming model uses only the six constructs and the closed type vocabulary,
satisfies the naming rules, has expressions/action bodies per sections 6-7, and
produces zero error-severity diagnostics. A conforming validator accepts all
conforming models, rejects non-conforming ones with at least one error, and
never executes model-supplied strings.

================================================================================
11. COMPLETE WORKED EXAMPLE (validates cleanly: 7 constructs, 0 warnings)
================================================================================
# Two services and a shared datastore; the rule "only one writer at a time"
# must hold across a platform cutover.

entity Service:
  description: A deployable software service (any language or runtime)
  identity: [serviceId]
  properties:
    serviceId: { type: string, required: true }
    name:      { type: string, required: true }
    platform:  { type: enum, values: [legacy, target] }

entity Datastore:
  description: A store of record the services read and write
  identity: [datastoreId]
  properties:
    datastoreId: { type: string, required: true }
    name:        { type: string, required: true }

relationship writes:
  from: Service
  to: Datastore
  cardinality: many

state DatastoreState:
  entity: Datastore
  dimensions:
    writeAuthority: { type: ref, target: Service }
    activeWriters:  { type: int }

invariant SingleWriter:
  appliesTo: Datastore
  expression:
    operator: "<="
    left: { function: count, path: activeWriters }
    right: 1
  severity: critical
  onViolation:
    action: block_transition

action TransferWriteAuthority:
  inputs:
    datastore: { type: ref, target: Datastore }
    source:    { type: ref, target: Service }
    target:    { type: ref, target: Service }
  preconditions:
    - datastore.activeWriters == 1
  effects:
    - datastore.writeAuthority = target
  rollback:
    - datastore.writeAuthority = source

transition PlatformCutover:
  action: TransferWriteAuthority
  from: { platform: legacy }
  to:   { platform: target }
  preserves:
    - SingleWriter

================================================================================
12. WORKFLOW FOR GENERATING WORLDSPEC
================================================================================
1. Emit ONLY valid WorldSpec YAML (no prose, no code fences).
2. Validate:  worldspec validate <dir>  (or the model JSON Schema for structure).
3. If there are WS-SYN-*/WS-SEM-* diagnostics, fix and regenerate.
The reference implementation and JSON Schema:
  https://github.com/banuap/worldspec
  https://w3id.org/worldspec/schemas/worldspec-model-0.1.schema.json
